title: Extract call_targets/output.rs — BGZF VCF writing and indexing
description: Move CallTargetsOutputState, WriteMetrics, begin_call_targets_output, write_call_targets_output_chunk, finish_call_targets_output, write_vcf_header, write_vcf_record, choose_alt, and base_index into src/call_targets/output.rs. This module owns the stateful VCF writer: it opens the BGZF file, writes the VCF header, iterates site counts to filter and emit records, and finalises the CSI or TBI index.
notes: choose_alt picks the highest-count non-ref allele across all samples. base_index maps A/C/G/T to 0-3. VCF records must be emitted in BTreeMap iteration order (sorted by SiteKey) — the CSI/TBI indexers require monotonically increasing coordinates. CSI and TBI indexers are mutually exclusive (only one is Some based on IndexType). WriteMetrics tracks ns-resolution timing for fasta_lookup, filter_eval, vcf_write, and index_update stages.

title: Extract call_targets/reference.rs — indexed FASTA random access
description: Move FaiRecord, FastaIndex, FastaReader, FastaLineCache, open_fai, open_fasta_index, and is_bgzf_reference into src/call_targets/reference.rs. FastaIndex provides line-cached random base lookup (fetch_base) into either a plain or BGZF-indexed FASTA. It is used exclusively by CallTargetsOutputState in output.rs to look up reference bases when writing VCF records.
notes: FastaIndex.line_cache stores the last-fetched FASTA line (ref_name + line_index + bases Vec). The cache hit path avoids a seek entirely. FastaIndex is only used in output.rs — make it pub(super) and import it from there.

title: Extract call_targets/pileup.rs — BAM scanning and count aggregation
description: Move ScanParams, PileupSettings, InputResult, process_input_bam, pileup_record, should_skip_record, record_sample, merge_counts, and merge_sample_counts_with_cap into src/call_targets/pileup.rs. This is the largest and most logic-dense grouping — it owns all BAM read iteration, CIGAR walking, base quality filtering, depth capping, and multi-BAM count merging.
notes: merge_sample_counts_with_cap uses proportional truncation with largest-remainder rounding — no behaviour changes allowed. ScanParams and PileupSettings hold references so they have lifetime parameters; keep them in this file. InputResult.skipped_rg and .skipped_flags are only used for verbose logging in run() — they must remain on the struct.

title: Extract call_targets/targets.rs — BED interval loading
description: Move load_targets and in_targets into src/call_targets/targets.rs. load_targets parses a BED file into a TargetIndex (HashMap<ref_id, Vec<Interval>> with merged intervals). in_targets binary-searches a sorted Vec<Interval> for a 0-based position.
notes: in_targets is called from pileup_record in pileup.rs — it will need to be pub(crate) or pub(super) depending on module visibility chosen.

title: Extract call_targets/types.rs — shared data model
description: Move SiteKey, SiteCounts, Interval, TargetIndex, and PreparedCallTargets out of call_targets.rs into src/call_targets/types.rs. These structs have no logic — they are pure data and are referenced across all other sub-modules, so they must be extracted first to avoid circular dependencies.
notes: All five types are currently pub(crate). Mark them pub(crate) in types.rs and re-export via mod.rs if needed. SiteKey derives Ord/Hash so BTreeMap and HashMap keying works — preserve all derives.

title: Extract call_targets/samples.rs — RG/SM resolution
description: Move collect_samples, build_rg_to_sm_from_map, rg_to_sm_from_header, read_rg_map, and insert_rg_sample into src/call_targets/samples.rs. These functions form a cohesive unit: given BAM paths and an optional rg-map file, they produce a (Vec<String>, HashMap<String,String>) of (sample_names, rg_to_sm).
notes: insert_rg_sample is called by both collect_samples (header path) and build_rg_to_sm_from_map (file path). Keep it in this module as pub(super).

title: Refactor call_targets.rs into focused sub-modules
description:
call_targets.rs is ~1450 lines and mixes eight distinct concerns in a single file. Split it into a call_targets/ directory module so each concern lives in its own file. The orchestration (run, prepare_call_targets) stays in mod.rs; everything else moves to purpose-named sub-modules.

Target structure:
  src/call_targets/
    mod.rs        — run(), prepare_call_targets(), write_call_targets_output()
    types.rs      — SiteKey, SiteCounts, Interval, TargetIndex, PreparedCallTargets
    samples.rs    — collect_samples, rg_to_sm_from_header, read_rg_map, insert_rg_sample, build_rg_to_sm_from_map
    targets.rs    — load_targets, in_targets
    pileup.rs     — ScanParams, PileupSettings, InputResult, process_input_bam, pileup_record, should_skip_record, record_sample, merge_counts, merge_sample_counts_with_cap
    reference.rs  — FaiRecord, FastaIndex, FastaReader, FastaLineCache, open_fai, open_fasta_index, is_bgzf_reference
    output.rs     — CallTargetsOutputState, WriteMetrics, begin_call_targets_output, write_call_targets_output_chunk, finish_call_targets_output, write_vcf_header, write_vcf_record, choose_alt, base_index

No behaviour changes — only file organisation.
design: Convert src/call_targets.rs to src/call_targets/mod.rs and extract each concern into a sibling file. Use pub(super) for items only needed within the call_targets module tree, pub(crate) for items referenced from outside (currently: run, prepare_call_targets, write_call_targets_output, begin_call_targets_output, write_call_targets_output_chunk, finish_call_targets_output, CallTargetsOutputState, SiteKey, SiteCounts, TargetIndex, PreparedCallTargets). Verify with cargo build and cargo clippy after each file extraction.
